scp.definition: All SCP articles from the main blocks should be tagged with scp , including translated SCP articles from other branches , 001 proposals , and joke (-J), archived (-ARC) and explained (-EX) SCPs. Articles using the SCP format but posted outside the main blocks as a tale may be tagged with foundation-format instead.

goi-format.definition: Written from the perspective of a specific Group of Interest, and conforms to the format or formats laid out in that GoI's hub page. GoI Formats should also be tagged with the tag of their GoI, as well as the underscore tag for that group's GoI format.

tale.definition: Any fiction that is set in the SCP universe but is not an SCP, GoI Format, site page or supplement.

supplement.definition: Pages which require context from their parent page to understand. These pages should also have their parent page set to their originating article, in order to maintain relevance and automatically create breadcrumb navigation. A page should only be tagged as supplement if they were created by the original author of the page, or with the original author's permission. Documents that are tributes or parodies should be tagged as tale instead, even if they are in the format of a log or transcript.

site.definition: Documentation of a specific Foundation facility, providing information such as the objects stored at the site, site history, and assigned personnel. A page tagged site that is also a hub page e.g. for SCPs contained within that site or for tales that take place there should also be tagged hub .

guide.definition: These pages are writing guides, helpful hints, or other pages intended to assist site users. Guides are only tagged by staff, or with staff permission. See also essay .

essay.definition: These pages contain useful information on specific topics that may be helpful to authors writing related articles, but are different from a guide in that essays are subjective and reflect the author's personal opinion.

news.definition: Contain news about the SCP Wiki in the broadest sense of the word, ranging from new artwork by fans to recently posted successful articles and everything in between. May also be used for formal announcements by staff (in as far as those aren't done on the main page or in the forum).

resource.definition: Pages listing various aspects of the SCP canon. May also adding information regarding them.

author.definition: This tag should be used for author profile pages. There should only be a single author profile page for each qualifying site member.

artist.definition: This tag should be used for artist profile pages. If a site member wants more than one artist profile page, they must get staff permission first.

artwork.definition: Used to host artwork created by community members, whether as individual pieces or collections.

component.definition: A page that is designed to be imported onto other pages via the [[include]] syntax in order to add new design, style or functionality.

component-backend.definition: An 'internal template' page that is designed only to be imported into other component or component-backend pages, but not used directly by authors.

theme.definition: Templates that modify the general look and feel of a page.

fragment.definition: A page that is designed to be imported onto a single page, containing content for that page only. They should be parented to that page. Pages of this type should be in the fragment: category and are exempt from deletion as long as their parent exists on the wiki. If they are orphaned or their parent is deleted, they are eligible for summary deletion.

more-by.definition: A page that lists works by a single author, designed to be imported onto that author's published works on the Wiki. They should be parented to that author's author page, if they have one. Pages of this type should be in the more-by: category and are exempt from voting. EN recommends that other branches do not translate these pages.

forum.definition: Pages required for internal functioning of Wikidot's forum system. They should only be created by staff.

splash.definition: Pages used as 'cover pages' for certain articles. (see: SCP-902 » SCP-902 Warning .)

redirect.definition: Pages that redirect the user to a different page. They should only be created by staff or with explicit staff permission.

admin.definition: These pages, as the name suggests, have some sort of official significance. Typically, they represent pages which are required to exist by Wikidot for some form of site functionality. They should only be created by staff.

hub.definition: Pages with a large number of links to related pages. Unlike other top level tags, hub may be applied alone or alongside another top level tag, if a page of that type also functions as a hub page. Hubs for series should also be tagged series-hub , canon hubs should be tagged canon-hub , hubs for specific Groups (GoI, Departments, MTFs) should be tagged group-hub , and hubs for contests should be tagged contest .

workbench.definition: Pages created by staff to allow them to monitor certain pages using Wikidot's modules. While you're free to use those same monitors on your author page, please do not create any workbench pages unless you're an (acting) team captain or admin , or have received explicit permission from them to do so.

sandbox.definition: In the very distant past, sandboxes on the main site were allowed. This was before the number of members became so big that the amount of sandboxes would create a total mess on the wiki. Please note that personal sandboxes are no longer allowed on the main site. The SCP Sandbox wiki at https://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/ should be used for article drafts. Only staff may authorize new sandboxes on the site.

001-proposal.definition: For articles proposing a possible SCP-001 object. SCPs tagged with this tag should also be tagged with their standard object class tag, or esoteric-class if applicable.

archived.definition: Pages which have been archived due to having been superseded, obsoleted, or inactivity. These pages should also be moved to the archived: category so they retain the 'archived page' notice and are locked from edits. See Archived Pages for more information on archival.

canon-hub.definition: Used for hubs which represent a canon .

co-authored.definition: Pages that are the result of two or more authors working together, but are not open to additions from other members (unless otherwise specified).

collaboration.definition: Pages that are or were open to additions from any site member, with the caveat that staff and the original author reserve the right to modify or delete entries found to be subpar, or close entries entirely.

contest.definition: Pages created for the purpose of community contests. This should be used in conjunction with hub in the case of entry listings, and should not be used on the entries themselves. These contests are only created by staff or with staff permission.

explained.definition: Explained (-EX) SCPs are a special type of scp , although explained can be used as an object class on any SCP. Articles that use Explained as an Object Class should be tagged with their previous object class tag, if it is included in the article.

group-hub.definition: Utilized in conjunction with the hub tag to designate that this hub represents a specific Group of Interest , Department or Mobile Task Force . May also be a canon-hub or series-hub .

joke.definition: Pages which are considered 'jokes' or explicitly 'out-of-universe'. Joke pages can be parodies of any page type, and should be tagged as such a page usually would. See Joke SCPs for more information on Joke pages.

policy.definition: Page contains actionable site rules and policy.

required.definition: Guides that are part of the required reading for joining the SCP Wiki.

series-hub.definition: Utilized in conjunction with the hub tag to designate that this hub represents a series of connected articles, which may include tales, SCPs and GoI Formats.

experiment.definition: This supplement contains primarily experiment logs (see: SCP-914 » Experiment Log 914 ). This tag should not be used on scp pages, even if they have extensive experiment logs.

exploration.definition: This supplement contains primarily exploration logs. This tag should not be used on scp pages, even if they have extensive exploration logs.

incident.definition: This supplement contains primarily incident logs or transcripts (see: SCP-555 » Incident Log 555-1 ). This tag should not be used on scp pages, even if they have extensive incident logs.

interview.definition: This supplement contains primarily interview transcripts (see: SCP-277 » Interview 277-A ). This tag should not be used on scp pages, even if they have extensive interview transcripts.

audio.definition: audio pages are pages with one or more audio files attached.

comic.definition: For stories that are primarily told via sequential art.

illustrated.definition: An article with one or more illustrations specifically made for it. This includes photobashes and other forms of photo manipulation that go beyond basic edits.

interactive.definition: The article contains elements that require user input or interaction, e.g. making a decision (making the user click a single link or open a collapsible does not count).

poetry.definition: Applicable if the page consists significantly of a form of poetry, with elements such as meter and rhyme.

video.definition: video pages are pages with one or more video files attached.

_adult.definition: Denotes mature content, readers aged 18 or over should view at their own discretion. Content tagged as adult should be given an Adult Content Warning , as explained in the Adult Content Warning Guide .

_listpages.definition: A tag to denote that this page is an LPT article. LPT (ListPages Presented Text) means that the article presents its content through offsets served via ListPages.

esoteric-class.definition: SCP has an object class other than one of the above.

adaptive.definition: SCP adapts to stimuli in an anomalous manner.

alive.definition: SCP is alive, meaning it exhibits the biological traits of metabolic activity and response to stimuli.

amorphous.definition: SCP lacks a set form or shape, constantly changes shape, or has a poorly-defined shape e.g. a blob.

autonomous.definition: SCP activates/operates on its own in an anomalous fashion. Only applicable to objects that normally do not activate on their own.

bacteria.definition: SCP is, resembles, or exhibits traits of a bacterial life-form.

child.definition: SCP is a child, or is related to or affects children.

clown.definition: SCP is, resembles, affects, or involves one or more clowns.

deity.definition: SCP is a deity or specifically affects a specific deity, or deities in general.

foundation-personnel.definition: SCP was employed by the Foundation prior to receiving an SCP designation, or was recruited as an employee after being contained. Also applies to anomalies that specifically affect one or more Foundation personnel.

fungus.definition: SCP is or exhibits traits of fungal organisms, including mushrooms, yeasts and mold.

hive-mind.definition: SCP exhibits traits of an external hive-mind, with multiple entities sharing a collective consciousness.

hostile.definition: SCP exhibits hostility towards humans or the Foundation that is not motivated by self-defence or the need for food.

humanoid.definition: SCP is humanoid or exhibits traits of humanoids.

metamorphic.definition: SCP changes its form or shape, often to mimic the form or shape of other objects or entities. Often used to describe shapeshifters or mimics.

nocturnal.definition: SCP is triggered by, activated by, or otherwise is only significantly active at night.

predatory.definition: SCP exhibits predatory behavior, obtaining food or sustenance by hunting for prey.

plant.definition: SCP is a plant or exhibits plant-like traits, particularly the use of photosynthesis to convert light into usable energy.

sapient.definition: SCP demonstrates sapience, the capacity for reason, abstract thought and complex emotional responses. Used to indicate intelligence similar to or exceeding human intelligence.

species.definition: SCP describes an entire species, rather than one or more individuals.

tree.definition: SCP is or exhibits traits of tree-like (arboreal) plants.

virus.definition: SCP exhibits traits of viral agents.

animal.definition: SCP is an animal or affects animal life, defined as mobile multicellular organisms with a fixed bodily structure, that must ingest their food rather than producing it through photosynthesis.

amphibian.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles amphibians, including frogs, toads and salamanders.

arachnid.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles spider-like animals, including scorpions, mites and ticks.

arthropod.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles arthropods, animals with exoskeletons, segmented bodies, and jointed appendages.

bear.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles bears.

bee.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles bees.

bird.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles birds.

bovine.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles cattle-like animals, including buffalo and bison.

butterfly.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles lepidopteran insects, including butterflies, moths, and skippers.

canine.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles dog-like animals, including wolves, coyote and jackals.

cephalopod.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles cephalopods, including the cuttlefish, octopus, and squid.

cetacean.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles whales, dolphins or porpoises.

crustacean.definition: SCP is, affects or is related to crustaceans, the family of arthropods that includes crabs, lobsters, shrimp, isopods and barnacles.

deer.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles deer, including elk and moose.

dinosaur.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles non-avian dinosaurs. Includes pterosaurs and extinct marine reptiles from the same era.

dragon.definition: SCP is, affects, or resembles dragons. Includes all varieties of dragon from various traditions.

equine.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles horse-like animals, including zebra and donkeys.

feline.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles cat-like animals, including big cats like lions and tigers.

fish.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles fish (piscine).

insect.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles insects, which are six-legged, three-segment arthropods. (This does not include spiders, which should be tagged arachnid , or woodlice, which should be tagged crustacean . This also does not include centipedes or millipedes, which should be tagged arthropod ).

invertebrate.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles invertebrates, animals lacking spinal columns.

primate.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles non-human apes, monkeys or lemurs.

pig.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles pigs (porcine).

rabbit.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles rabbits or hares.

reptile.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles reptiles.

rodent.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles rodents, including mice, rats, squirrels and beavers.

shark.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles sharks, including rays and skates.

snake.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles snakes.

worm.definition: SCP is, affects or resembles worms.

biological.definition: SCP consists of biological molecules, tissues or organs, but is not a living organism.

biohazard.definition: SCP poses a hazard to humans or the environment as a result of biological function, or is a biological hazard.

blood.definition: SCP is related to or anomalously affects blood.

cadaver.definition: SCP is, affects, or is related to corpses, carcasses, or any dead biological matter.

contagion.definition: SCP is a contagious agent or exhibits contagious spread.

dental.definition: SCP is or deals with teeth or the practice of dentistry.

extremity.definition: SCP is or affects specific extremities, regardless of species.

genetic.definition: SCP is related to or functions primarily via genetic encoding and decoding.

medical.definition: SCP is used or intended for use as a medical device or otherwise has medical significance outside of pathology.

ocular.definition: SCP resembles, is related to, or affects eyes.

parasitic.definition: SCP exhibits traits of parasitic or parasitoid organisms.

reanimation.definition: SCP has been restored or restores others, either whole or in part, to life from a clinically dead state.

reproductive.definition: SCP anomalously affects or interferes with the normal biological reproductive processes of an organism (which may include humans), or has anomalous biological reproductive methods other than that covered by parasitic or self-replicating .

sexual.definition: SCP includes or affects sexual organs, sexual activities, or concepts related to sex. Does not imply that the work is intended for adult audiences only.

skeletal.definition: SCP is a skeleton, consists of bones or cartilage from a skeleton, or affects the skeletal tissue of an organism.

toxic.definition: SCP induces chemical toxicity in subjects.

addictive.definition: SCP causes desire or addictive behavior in affected subjects.

antimemetic.definition: SCP is or involves the use of an antimeme to suppress knowledge or understanding of an idea or concept, including itself.

auditory.definition: SCP is triggered by, activated by, or affects the sense of hearing.

cognitohazard.definition: SCP is hazardous to subjects when observed by any of the traditional 5 senses.

compulsion.definition: SCP induces compulsive behavior in subjects, or otherwise causes them to do things they normally would not do. This is usually a temporary urge following exposure to an anomaly, rather than a permanent change to behavior.

dream.definition: SCP affects or is related to dreaming.

empathic.definition: SCP is capable of reading or conveying emotion, feelings, or sensations.

gustatory.definition: SCP is triggered by, activated by, or affects the sense of taste.

hallucination.definition: SCP induces hallucinations in subjects or is a hallucination.

infohazard.definition: SCP has an effect that is triggered when it is described or referred to. Unlike a meme, an infohazard is an object that has effects on thoughts and behavior related to it.

knowledge.definition: SCP absorbs, stores, generates, grants, or possesses knowledge that is anomalous, or through an anomalous process.

language.definition: SCP imparts, exhibits, or is an anomalous language.

memetic.definition: SCP is a memetic agent or exhibits traits of memetic propagation , meaning that the information itself is anomalous and infectious.

memory-affecting.definition: SCP imparts, changes, or removes memories in intelligent beings such as human subjects.

mind-affecting.definition: SCP affects the thinking of subjects, which can include behavior and overall intelligence. This change may be permanent, and may make people do things they would otherwise never do.

neurological.definition: SCP directly or indirectly affects the neurological system of subjects. Only applicable in situations where objective neurological changes are confirmed, as opposed to a simple change of behavior in the subject.

observational.definition: SCP has properties that are triggered or suppressed when directly or indirectly observed. These properties may or may not also be cognitohazards .

olfactory.definition: SCP exhibits anomalous scent, is propagated through smell, or affects the sense of smelling.

predictive.definition: SCP is capable of imparting information or acting in a predictive manner.

sleep.definition: SCP affects or is related to sleep.

tactile.definition: SCP is activated by, triggered by, or affects the sense of touch. Note that this refers specifically to the sense of touch, not simply something that can activate by touching something else.

telepathic.definition: SCP can transfer information via anomalous means.

visual.definition: SCP is triggered by, activated by, or affects the sense of sight.

acoustic.definition: SCP is composed of, affects, generates, or is propagated by sound waves.

chemical.definition: SCP is an anomalous chemical compound or produces chemical compounds in an anomalous way.

ectoentropic.definition: SCP produces energy or matter in an unexplained way, violating the principle that entropy (the disorder of a system) must always increase.

electrical.definition: SCP generates, stores, or uses electric energy.

electromagnetic.definition: SCP generates, stores, or uses electromagnetic energy or fields.

entropic.definition: SCP has anomalous properties that affect or are affected by entropy, or that resemble entropic decay.

fire.definition: SCP is composed of fire, is related to fire, or enables the process of combustion.

future.definition: SCP is from the future or was otherwise temporally displaced from a later time frame into an earlier one.

gaseous.definition: SCP is or generates a gas.

gravity.definition: SCP has anomalous properties that affect or are significantly affected by gravitational force.

immobile.definition: SCP cannot be moved via any means known by the Foundation.

indestructible.definition: SCP cannot be destroyed via any means known by the Foundation.

intangible.definition: SCP is intangible in a manner that precludes definition as a gas.

light.definition: SCP is triggered, activated, or propagated by or otherwise related to the presence or absence of visible light.

liquid.definition: SCP is or generates a liquid.

loop.definition: SCP exhibits or is engaged in a repeating loop of activity.

magnetic.definition: SCP is magnetic or exhibits traits of magnetic charge and/or attraction.

microscopic.definition: SCP is too small to be seen or otherwise meaningfully observed by the naked eye.

miniature.definition: SCP is much smaller than objects it resembles.

mobile.definition: SCP is capable of movement by anomalous means. Generally only applies to objects or entities that are normally incapable of movement.

portal.definition: SCP is an opening or doorway that exhibits inconsistent connectivity.

paradox.definition: SCP generates, exhibits, or is involved in paradoxical events or situations.

polyhedral.definition: SCP is a polyhedron: a three-dimensional shape with flat faces and straight edges.

radioactive.definition: SCP emits radiation or affects radioactivity.

reality-bending.definition: SCP is capable of manipulating reality through anomalous means (aka 'ontokinetic', 'reality-bending' or 'reality warping') .

self-repairing.definition: SCP exhibits an anomalous ability to restore or repair itself to functional condition.

self-replicating.definition: SCP exhibits an anomalous ability to replicate other than via biological reproduction.

shadow.definition: SCP is triggered, activated, or otherwise significantly affected by shadows cast by subjects.

spatial.definition: SCP warps or distorts space, creating phenomena like impossible geometries or spaces that are larger or smaller than they should be.

telekinetic.definition: SCP can manipulate objects or initiate or affect reactions through anomalous means.

teleportation.definition: SCP can move without occupying intervening space or otherwise displace itself via anomalous means that preclude normal movement.

temporal.definition: SCP affects or is otherwise related to the passage of time.

thaumaturgy.definition: SCP is, performs, or is otherwise related to thaumaturgy.

thermal.definition: SCP is activated by, triggered by, or propagates via changes in temperature. This includes both increases and decreases in temperature.

thermodynamic.definition: SCP affects, or is otherwise significantly related to the laws of thermodynamics.

transfiguration.definition: SCP alters the shape or substance of subjects in an anomalous manner.

transmission.definition: SCP transmits information, typically via radio waves or other electromagnetic means.

vibration.definition: SCP generates anomalous vibrations or other repetitive movement.

airborne.definition: SCP lives in, moves through, or spreads through the air.

aquatic.definition: SCP lives in, moves through or spreads through water.

building.definition: SCP is, is a part of, or otherwise is related to or affects a building, defined as a structure intended for human occupation.

city.definition: SCP is a city, town, or other settlement intended for human occupation. Should only be used if a significant portion of the city is considered anomalous.

extradimensional.definition: SCP is of extradimensional origin, moves or propagates extradimensionally, and/or enables extradimensional travel.

extraterrestrial.definition: SCP originates or is found in outer space and/or enables or exhibits extraterrestrial travel.

forest.definition: SCP is or affects forests, groves, orchards or other locations with high amounts of trees, manmade or otherwise.

geological.definition: SCP is or affects natural geological formations (such as caves, cliffs, mountains, valleys, or volcanoes) or significant geological events (such as earthquakes, landslides, or sinkholes).

island.definition: SCP is or affects islands, archipelagos or similar formations.

library.definition: SCP is, or is otherwise related to or affects libraries, defined as a room or building containing books for people to read or borrow.

location.definition: SCP is a unique geographical location.

meteorological.definition: SCP is, affects, or is affected by significant weather events.

moon.definition: SCP is, is related to, or affects a natural satellite or moon, which may or may not be our moon.

planet.definition: SCP is, resembles, or affects a planet, which may or may not be our planet.

satellite.definition: SCP is a small natural or artificial satellite orbiting Earth or another celestial body.

structure.definition: SCP is an artificial or natural structure. If it is intended for human occupation, it should be tagged building instead.

subterranean.definition: SCP is located or operates wholly or significantly underground, defined as below the surface of the Earth. Does not apply objects that are simply contained underground for safety or convenience.

sun.definition: SCP is, is related to, or affects a sun, which may or may not be our sun.

artifact.definition: SCP is an object created by humans (or other intelligent beings).

archaeological.definition: SCP is an object uncovered in an archaeological dig, or otherwise relates to the material culture of an ancient people.

alcohol.definition: SCP is, affects or is related to alcoholic beverages, alcohol production and distribution, or alcohol intoxication. Alcohols other than ethanol are excluded.

appliance.definition: SCP is an artifact that is or that resembles a household or domestic appliance.

artistic.definition: SCP is a work of art, exhibits artistic properties, or has artistic significance.

clockwork.definition: SCP operates primarily via clockworks, defined as mechanical devices constructed primarily using toothed wheels and/or powered by a mainspring.

clothing.definition: SCP is an artifact primarily intended to be worn for protection or comfort, including armor and protective helmets.

cinema.definition: SCPs that are, relate to or concern the medium of cinema, film and/or movies.

computer.definition: SCP is a computational device, an artifact that can carry out mathematical operations, or affects computational devices.

currency.definition: SCP is, is related to, affects, or is activated by money or other objects primarily intended to be used as a medium of exchange.

document.definition: SCP is a textual document. This category of artifact includes books.

electronic.definition: SCP is an artifact that operates using semiconductors (such as the ones in computer chips) to control the flow of electricity within it, or resembles an electronic device.

food.definition: SCP is intended to be an edible object or substance, including any artifacts classed as food or drink.

foundation-made.definition: SCP was created, intentionally or otherwise, by the SCP Foundation itself. Note that only the objects whose creation resulted directly from specific actions of the Foundation are applicable. This excludes the spontaneous occurrence of anomalous phenomena at Foundation sites containing a large number of anomalies.

game.definition: SCP is, is used in, is activated by, or is otherwise related to one or more games, defined as a method of play or a sport — especially a competitive one — that can be won or lost by skill, strength, or luck.

inscription.definition: SCP features carvings, engravings or inscriptions of words, numbers or other designs.

jewelry.definition: SCP is an artifact intended to be worn in a decorative manner.

mechanical.definition: SCP operates primarily in a mechanical manner or is a mechanical device.

media.definition: SCP is or is recorded onto one or more artifacts consisting of fixed or removable analog or digital media, such as a tape, record, disc, or drive.

musical.definition: SCP is, generates, or is generated by musical compositions.

online.definition: SCP is available on, utilizes, or is dependent on a connection to the internet.

performance.definition: SCP is or is used in a performance art, such as a dance or play.

photographic.definition: SCP is or deals with photography or features a photograph as part of the primary anomaly.

robotic.definition: SCP is a device or construct designed to operate on its own. Applicable to anything that can be described as a 'robot' or 'automaton'.

sculpture.definition: SCP is a three-dimensional piece of artwork. To be considered a sculpture , the object in question must have been formed, carved, or molded.

sport.definition: SCP is, is used in, is activated by, or is otherwise related to one or more sports, defined as an activity involving physical exertion and skill in which one or more individuals compete against other individuals or teams.

tool.definition: SCP is an artifact intended to be used as a tool.

toy.definition: SCP is an artifact intended to be used as a toy.

weapon.definition: SCP is an artifact intended to be used as a weapon.

vehicle.definition: SCP is an artifact intended to be used as a vehicle.

anomalous-event.definition: The SCP is a singular anomalous event that significantly changed reality in some way.

concept.definition: SCP is or affects a purely non-physical concept. Should not be used in conjunction with tags describing physical qualities.

exchange.definition: SCP involves or is triggered by some form of exchange of physical or intangible goods, including but not limited to bartering and monetary trade.

k-class-scenario.definition: SCP is involved in or capable of instigating one or more K-Class End of the World scenarios. Should only be used in articles that specifically name a type of K-Class scenario.

mathematical.definition: SCP is or affects a number, number system, mathematical equation, or otherwise has mathematical significance.

meta.definition: SCP affects or is affected by its documentation or requires containment procedures regarding the way it is documented.

military.definition: SCP is military equipment, part or member of a military unit, or otherwise has military significance.

narrative.definition: SCP is, is related to, or propagates via narrative information or documents.

probability.definition: SCP alters or manipulates the probability of certain events or outcomes.

phenomenon.definition: The SCP is an ongoing widespread phenomenon not directly linked to a physical source.

religious.definition: SCP is associated with or otherwise has significance to one or more organized religions.

ritual.definition: SCP is or is triggered or activated through ritualistic behavior.

uncontained.definition: SCP is neither fully nor partially contained. This should only be applied to SCPs where no instances are contained at all and does not apply if the Foundation successfully contains one or more instances.

absurdism.definition: Absurdist fiction depicts events lacking any rational explanation or meaning, for the purpose of shock or comedy.

action.definition: Focuses on exciting events and spectacular violence.

adventure.definition: Focuses on the exploration of unfamiliar environments.

bittersweet.definition: Intended to evoke both positive and negative emotions.

bleak.definition: Intended to evoke negative emotions, focusing on anguish, distress and suffering.

breakout.definition: Focuses on containment breaches and escaping from imprisonment, both attempted and successful.

black-comedy.definition: Depicts shocking or unpleasant situations for the purpose of humour.

crime-fiction.definition: Focuses on criminals and law enforcement.

fantasy.definition: Depicts magic and the supernatural, inspired by myths, legends and folklore.

heartwarming.definition: Intended to evoke positive emotions, focusing on pleasant experiences and supportive relationships.

horror.definition: Intended to evoke fear, shock or disgust in the reader.

body-horror.definition: Focuses on physical mutilation or transformation.

cosmic-horror.definition: Focuses on human insignificance and irrelevance, with emphasis on the unknowable and incomprehensible.

psychological-horror.definition: Focuses on psychological distress and emotional vulnerability.

metafiction.definition: Fiction about fiction, referencing the work's fictional nature. May feature fiction affecting reality, characters entering or leaving works of fiction, and characters being aware that they are fictional or are influenced by narrative tropes.

military-fiction.definition: Focuses on soldiers, field agents or mercenaries.

mystery.definition: Focuses on investigation of unanswered questions.

religious-fiction.definition: Focuses on religion, faith and belief. Includes depictions of real-world religions, as well as worship of anomalous entities.

romance.definition: Focuses on romantic relationships between characters.

science-fiction.definition: Depicts advanced science and technology. May feature scientific analysis of phenomena currently thought to be impossible.

slice-of-life.definition: Offers a look into the everyday lives of characters, with a focus on the mundane.

spy-fiction.definition: Focuses on espionage, counter-intelligence, double agents or assassination.

surrealism.definition: Surrealist fiction creates a dream-like story by juxtaposing the mundane with the impossible and irrational, with an emphasis on subconscious associations between them.

xenofiction.definition: Told from a non-human perspective, such as an alien, an animal, a monster or a machine.

alien.definition: Features extraterrestrial life, typically intelligent beings originating from a planet other than Earth.

artificial-intelligence.definition: Features artificial intelligence, and on anomalies that have AI or affect AIs.

bureaucracy.definition: Focuses on the administration, management and official procedures of an organisation.

demon.definition: Features supernatural entities traditionally associated with evil, temptation and Hell.

faerie.definition: Features fairies, fae or the fair folk, supernatural entities inspired by European folklore.

ghost.definition: Features ghosts, the spirits of the dead, often referred to as spectral entities by the Foundation.

historical.definition: Focuses on historical events, or on anomalies with historical significance.

journalism.definition: Focuses on journalists, professional reviewers, news reports or news articles.

legal.definition: Focuses on law, legal proceedings, and legal documents.

lgbtq.definition: Focuses on LGBTQ+ themes, culture or history. Possible examples include portrayal of gay and lesbian relationships, depiction of asexuality and bisexuality, and exploration of trans, queer and agender identities.

murder-monster.definition: Features an inhuman anomaly that is hostile to human life.

mythological.definition: Features creatures, events and practices drawn from a specific mythology, outside of structured religious practice.

political.definition: Focuses on politicians, diplomats or the political process.

superhero.definition: Features superheroes or supervillains. These characters typically have secret identities, costumes and supernatural powers.

time-travel.definition: Features travel to or from the past or future.

afterlife.definition: Describes or is otherwise related to what happens to consciousness after death.

alternate-history.definition: Depicts historical events that diverge from how they happened in real life. Does not apply to future events, or to anomalous reinterpretations of events that did happen (e.g. the 7th Occult War).

apocalyptic.definition: Takes place during or shortly before a catastrophic event.

post-apocalyptic.definition: Takes place after a catastrophic event.

christmas.definition: Set during or influenced by the holiday of Christmas.

corporate.definition: Focuses on the operations of a business or corporation.

dystopian.definition: Imagines a non-existent society or culture, portrayed as being much worse than our current one.

halloween.definition: Set during or influenced by the holiday of Halloween.

otherworldly.definition: Set in another world, reality or universe. Includes works that are entirely set somewhere other than Earth, as well as stories featuring travel to another world.

period-piece.definition: Set during a specific period in the past.

school.definition: Focuses on a school, college or university, or on the education system more broadly.

space-opera.definition: Depicts a futuristic society in outer space or on other planets, focusing on dramatic events and adventures.

utopian.definition: Imagines a non-existent society or culture, portrayed as being much better than our current one.

western.definition: Set in the American Frontier, during the time period known as the Wild West.

correspondence.definition: Also known as epistolary, these stories are told in large part though written communications between characters, including letters, emails, text messages or social media posts.

creepypasta.definition: In the style of short, old-school Internet horror. Typically lacks any reference to the Foundation.

first-person.definition: Told from a first-person perspective ("I did").

foundation-format.definition: Told in the form of an SCP document.

journal.definition: Told in the form of an in-universe journal, written while events happen.

second-person.definition: Told from a second-person perspective ("you did").

worldbuilding.definition: Focuses on conveying information about the setting, often taking the form of an in-universe document.

_genreless.definition: No Genre, Theme, Setting or Style tags apply to this article.

3d-rendered.definition: Artwork is a 2D image created using 3D modelling software.

animated.definition: Artwork is animated. Generally applies to .gif files.

cel-shaded.definition: Artwork with outlines drawn, then shaded using solid blocks of color or gradients. The characteristic style of animation, comic books and manga.

colored-pencil.definition: Artwork colored by pencil or crayon, or digital art emulating that style.

image-editing.definition: Artwork created by altering or combining existing images.

inked.definition: Artwork coloured using marker pen, or other source of ink.

outlined.definition: Artwork representing the outline of an object in a single color, with minimal internal detail.

painted.definition: Artwork created using paint or pastel, or using digital brush strokes to blend colors and mimic the appearance of paint.

photographed.definition: Photograph of an artwork, usually a physical 3-dimensional object created by the artist. If another art style tag is applicable to an artwork, it should generally replace this tag.

pixel-art.definition: Artwork created by drawing individual pixels, or by deliberately pixelating an existing image.

sketched.definition: Artwork drawn with pencil, pen or crayon, or created digitally in a similar style. May be shaded, or left as a line drawing.

goi-art.definition: Artwork depicting characters, objects, or themes from a specific Group of Interest. Also applies to GoI logos.

logo.definition: Artwork depicting one or more logos or other symbolic graphics, such as insignia, flags and coats of arms.

personnel-art.definition: Artwork depicting specific SCP Foundation employees.

poster.definition: Artwork depicting a poster, infographic or advertisement, using a combination of images and words to convey information.

scp-art.definition: Artwork depicting one or more SCP objects or entities.

tale-art.definition: Artwork created as a depiction of a specific tale on the SCP Wiki.

task-force-art.definition: Artwork depicting members of one of the Foundation's Mobile Task Forces, or their MTF insignia.

in-rewrite.definition: This is a temporary tag that may be placed on an article by members of the Rewrite Team only. It indicates the article is currently being rewritten and should be removed when said rewrite is posted. It should only be placed on articles where for reasons of site history, the original article will not be deleted before the rewrite is posted.
relations: by members of the Rewrite Team

rewritable.definition: This is a temporary tag that may be be placed on an article by members of the Rewrite Team only. It indicates that the article is eligible to be rewritten due to circumstances falling outside the usual case of low rating and old age.
relations: by members of the Rewrite Team

rewrite.definition: This tag indicates that the article in question is a rewrite of another article.

in-deletion.definition: This is a temporary tag that indicates the article is currently eligible for deletion. It should only be placed on articles where a deletion post has been made by staff. Other tags should be removed when this tag is added to a page. Remove if deletion is permanently cancelled for whatever reason.

deletable.definition: This is a temporary tag that indicates the article has been voted on for deletion. It should only be placed on articles where three members of staff have witnessed the article for deletion and any deletion timers have expired. Other tags should be removed when this tag is added to a page. Remove if deletion is permanently cancelled for whatever reason.

featured.definition: Designates Featured Articles and Featured Tales .

reviewers-spotlight.definition: Designates articles featured by a forum reviewer .

event-featured.definition: Designates articles featured on special event days such as International Talk Like a Pirate Day.

metadata.definition: Designates pages with meta information about articles.

unlisted.definition: Article has been unlisted from the SCP wiki.

_crom-hidden.definition: Article cannot be searched for by crom.

_cc.definition: This tag indicates that all images on the page in question have been demonstrated to be fully compliant with current licensing policy. This tag should only be added by a member of the Licensing team . This tag conflicts with _image.

_cc4.definition: This tag indicates that the page includes an image licensed under CC4. This tag should only be added by a member of the Licensing team . This tag conflicts with _image.

_image.definition: This is a temporary tag that the tagging team applies to new articles that include one or more images, irrespective of whatever licensing information is provided by the author. This tag acts as an indicator to the Licensing team that there are images whose license status need to be confirmed and/or verified. Licensing will remove the _image tag and replace it with the _cc tag (or, if images are removed, no tag at all) when appropriate.

_licensebox.definition: Used for pages which have a standard license info block.

_theme-temp.definition: Temporary tag used for marking old theme locations while they were moved from the component: category to the theme: category.
